commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1eff368a1758736337c64b051b12006468a46241)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds read-only warm MCP catalog projection to `tools.effective`, extends the effective-tool protocol source to `mcp`, and updates Control UI/docs/tests for MCP notices and grouping.

PR surface: Source +439, Tests +362, Docs +9. Total +810 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only permits `core`, `plugin`, and `channel` effective-tool sources, so warm MCP tools cannot be represented as `source: "mcp"`. I did not run a live MCP server in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor proof plus maintainer/API compatibility judgment, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary
- Shows MCP tools in `tools.effective` only after the session MCP runtime has already warmed a matching catalog.
- Keeps `tools.effective` read-only: no new refresh RPC, no transport startup, no MCP `tools/list` from dashboard reads.
- Adds notices for cold, not-yet-listed, and stale MCP catalog states, plus UI/docs/tests for the new `source: "mcp"` inventory group.

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related / Refs #76724
- Related #70876

## Motivation
MCP tools can already be available to an agent run after the session runtime lists them, but the Agent Tools panel only showed the static core/plugin/channel inventory. This keeps the dashboard read path cheap and safe while still reflecting MCP tools once a real agent turn has warmed the catalog.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: warm MCP tools appear in `tools.effective`; cold dashboard reads do not start/list MCP transports.

Real environment tested: local checkout on rewritten PR head `1eff368a17`, current `origin/main` base `4252f07ff0`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --reporter=verbose packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts --reporter=verbose ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config /tmp/openclaw-agents-mcp-tools.vitest.config.ts --reporter=verbose
/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-skills/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Evidence after fix:
- Gateway tests: 2 files passed, 94 tests passed.
- SDK e2e: 1 file passed, 4 passed, 1 live test skipped.
- UI panel test: 1 file passed, 7 tests passed.
- Agents inventory test: 1 file passed, 18 tests passed.
- MCP materialize/request-boundary focused config: 2 files passed, 10 tests passed.
- Autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix:
- Cold `tools.effective` returns base inventory plus MCP notices without creating/listing MCP runtimes.
- Warm matching MCP catalogs are projected as `source: "mcp"` after final policy and runtime schema quarantine.
- Sandboxed runtimes compare MCP config against the runtime workspace, not the parent workspace.
- Base inventory cache keys include the resolved workspace.

What was not tested:
- A live external MCP server/UI browser proof after dropping the refresh RPC.
- Full cross-platform/broad CI.

## Root Cause
`tools.effective` was a static inventory path, while bundled MCP tools are discovered later by the session MCP runtime. The dashboard and runtime could therefore diverge after MCP discovery.

## User-visible Behavior Changes
The Agent Tools panel can show MCP tools once an agent run has already warmed the session MCP catalog. Before that, it shows an explanatory notice instead of triggering MCP discovery itself.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No new calls from `tools.effective`; it only peeks existing runtime state.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.

## Compatibility / Migration
Backward compatible with current main. `tools.effective.refresh` was dropped from this unshipped PR rewrite and is not part of the current main Gateway protocol.
target.author: nxmxbbd

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
